metadata.preview:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30

display.text:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

state.title: .github/workflows/ci.yml

part.text: Now I understand the CI workflow. Let me look at the mise.toml to understand the envs and how they're defined, and the binding/example configurations.
Now let me understand the manifests. Let me look at `mise.toml`, the bindings, examples, and how envs are defined.

state.output:
<path>/app/mise.toml</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # https://mise.jdx.dev
2: # Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
3: # repository tasks.
4: 
5: experimental_monorepo_root = true
6: 
7: [settings]
8: lockfile = true
9: lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
10: pin = true
11: experimental = true
12: python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
13: windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
14: windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
15: unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
16: unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
17: # On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
18: # mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
19: activate_aggressive = true
20: 
21: [monorepo]
22: config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]
23: 
24: [tools]
25: # General tooling
26: "aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
27: "aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
28: "aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency

metadata.preview:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

display.text:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

[monorepo]
config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]

[tools]
# General tooling
"aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks
"aqua:jdx/hk" = "1.45.0" # Linter orchestration
"aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency
"aqua:dprint/dprint" = "0.53.2" # Formatter orchestration
"github:sargunv/jvl" = "2026.05.03" # JSON validator
"aqua:rhysd/actionlint" = "1.7.8" # GH Actions linter
"aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)

# C/C++ ecosystem
"github:prefix-dev/pixi" = "0.68.1"
"aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
"aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"
"conda:clang-format" = "22.1.7"
"conda:clang-tools" = "22.1.7"
"conda:doxygen" = "1.13.2"
"conda:glslang" = "16.3.0"
"conda:pkg-config" = "0.29.2"
"conda:vulkan-tools" = "1.4.341.0"
"github:cmakefmt/cmakefmt" = "1.3.0"
"aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }

# Node.js ecosystem
"core:node" = "24.11.0"
"aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"

# Java/Kotlin ecosystem
"core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
"aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
"github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"

# Python ecosystem
"core:python" = "3.14.4"
"aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"

# Swift ecosystem
"core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
"github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"

# Other ecosystems
"core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }
"core:zig" = "0.16.0"
"core:go" = "1.26.3"
"core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"

[env]
UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE = "{{env.MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE | default(value=\"RelWithDebInfo\")}}"
MLN_FFI_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/include"
MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/include"
MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"
MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS = { required = true }
MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"

[hooks]
postinstall = [
  { task = "sync-submodules" },
  "hk install --mise --quiet",
  "pixi install --locked --quiet",
  # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
  # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
  '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
  "uv sync --locked --quiet",
  "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
  "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
]

[tasks.configure]
depends = ["//:deps"]
run = [
  '''
  cmake_args=(
    -S .
    -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
    -G Ninja
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
    -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
    -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
    -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
  )

  while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
    [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
  done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"

  cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
  ''',
  '''
  if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
    mkdir -p build/clangd
    compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
    compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
    rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
    ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
    mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
  fi
  ''',
]

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:configure"]
run = '''
build_args=(
  --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
  --parallel
)

cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''

[tasks.clean]
run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""

[tasks.deps]
description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
run = '''
if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  mise run deps:angle
fi
'''

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:build"]
run = '''
(
  cd src/c_api/tests/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
(
  cd bindings/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
'''

[tasks.check]
run = "hk check --all"

[tasks.fix]
run = "hk fix --all"

[tasks.actions-up]
description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"

[tasks._ktfmt]
description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
hide = true
run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
# ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'

[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
  "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
  "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
]

[tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

part.text:
So the variant manifests exist in `.mise/config.*.toml`. Now I need to understand the per-component manifests (bindings and examples). The CI workflow runs tasks like `//bindings/java-ffm:build` for certain envs. The conditions in CI express "this binding/example is supported on these envs".

Let me look at the bindings and examples directories to see if there are manifests describing which envs/platforms they support.
Now let me look at the bindings and examples structure to understand component manifests.

state.output:
===== bindings/dotnet/mise.toml =====
[tasks.generate]
run = "scripts/generate-clangsharp.sh"

[tasks.build]
# Visual Studio exports Platform=x64 on Windows; the .NET solution uses Any CPU.
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = 'dotnet build Maplibre.Native.slnx -p:Platform="Any CPU"'

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = 'dotnet test bindings/dotnet/tests/Maplibre.Native.Tests/Maplibre.Native.Tests.csproj -p:Platform="Any CPU"'

[tasks.ci]
depends = [":test"]

===== bindings/java-ffm/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library", ":jextract:update-includes"]
run = "../../gradlew :bindings:java-ffm:build"

===== bindings/java-jni/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :bindings:java-jni:build"

===== bindings/kotlin-native/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :bindings:kotlin-native:build"

===== bindings/rust/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = "cargo build -p maplibre-native-sys -p maplibre-native-core -p maplibre-native"

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = "cargo test -p maplibre-native-sys -p maplibre-native-core -p maplibre-native -- --test-threads=1"

[tasks.ci]
depends = [":test"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = "cargo clippy -p maplibre-native-sys -p maplibre-native-core -p maplibre-native --all-targets -- -D warnings"

===== bindings/swift/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = 'swift build --scratch-path ".build/$MLN_FFI_VARIANT"'

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = 'swift test --scratch-path ".build/$MLN_FFI_VARIANT"'

[tasks.ci]
depends = ["test"]
run = 'swift package describe --type json >/dev/null'

===== bindings/zig/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "zig build {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }}"

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --summary all --verbose"

[tasks.ci]
depends = [":test"]

===== examples/dotnet-map/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = 'dotnet build examples/dotnet-map/Maplibre.Native.Examples.DotnetMap.csproj -p:Platform="Any CPU"'

[tasks.run]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
usage = 'arg "render-target"'
run = 'dotnet run --project examples/dotnet-map/Maplibre.Native.Examples.DotnetMap.csproj -p:Platform="Any CPU" -- "{{ usage.render_target }}"'

[tasks."run:owned-texture"]
run = [{ task = "run", args = ["owned-texture"] }]

[tasks."run:borrowed-texture"]
run = [{ task = "run", args = ["borrowed-texture"] }]

[tasks."run:native-surface"]
run = [{ task = "run", args = ["native-surface"] }]

===== examples/lwjgl-map/mise.toml =====
[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :examples:lwjgl-map:build"

[tasks.run]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
usage = 'arg "render-target"'
run = '../../gradlew :examples:lwjgl-map:run --args="{{ usage.render_target }}"'

[tasks."run:owned-texture"]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :examples:lwjgl-map:run --args='owned-texture'"

[tasks."run:borrowed-texture"]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :examples:lwjgl-map:run --args='borrowed-texture'"

[tasks."run:native-surface"]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
run = "../../gradlew :examples:lwjgl-map:run --args='native-surface'"

===== examples/rust-map/mise.toml =====
[tasks.compile-shaders]
hide = true
run = '''
set -euo pipefail

if [[ "${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-}" == "vulkan" ]]; then
  glslangValidator -V src/vulkan_texture_compositor/shaders/fullscreen.vert -o src/vulkan_texture_compositor/shaders/fullscreen.vert.spv
  glslangValidator -V src/vulkan_texture_compositor/shaders/sample.frag -o src/vulkan_texture_compositor/shaders/sample.frag.spv
fi
'''

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library", ":compile-shaders"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = '''
case "${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-}" in
  metal | opengl | vulkan) feature="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND" ;;
  *) echo "unsupported rust-map render backend: ${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-unset}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
esac
cargo build -p rust-map --no-default-features --features "$feature"
'''

[tasks.ci]
depends = [":build"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
run = '''
case "${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-}" in
  metal | opengl | vulkan) feature="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND" ;;
  *) echo "unsupported rust-map render backend: ${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-unset}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
esac
cargo clippy -p rust-map --no-default-features --features "$feature" --all-targets -- -D warnings
'''

[tasks.run]
depends = ["//:ensure-native-library", ":compile-shaders"]
dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
usage = 'arg "render-target"'
run = '''
case "${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-}" in
  metal | opengl | vulkan) feature="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND" ;;
  *) echo "unsupported rust-map render backend: ${MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND:-unset}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
esac
cargo run -p rust-map --no-default-features --features "$feature" -- "{{ usage.render_target }}"
'''

state.title: for f in bindings/*/mise.toml examples/*/mise.toml; do echo "===== $f ====="; cat "$f"; echo; done

part.text:
Now I have a clear picture. The CI workflow has:

1. A `variants` matrix job with `mise_env` -> `runner` mappings (11 variants).
2. Per-step conditions that gate which bindings/examples run on which env.

The CI conditions encode "CI target support policy" — which variants each binding/example supports. This is the "drifting" policy. Let me extract the rules:

**Variant matrix (mise_env -> runner):**
- linux-arm64-egl -> ubuntu-24.04-arm
- linux-arm64-vulkan -> ubuntu-24.04-arm
- linux-x64-egl -> ubuntu-latest
- linux-x64-vulkan -> ubuntu-latest
- macos-arm64-metal -> macos-latest
- macos-arm64-vulkan -> macos-latest
- macos-arm64-egl -> macos-latest
- ios-arm64-metal -> macos-latest
- ios-simulator-arm64-metal -> macos-latest
- windows-x64-vulkan -> windows-2022
- windows-x64-wgl -> windows-2022

**Steps and their conditions:**

1. `mise run configure` — always
2. Build native library — `startsWith(ios-)` 
3. Test native library — `!startsWith(ios-)`
4. Build Java FFM — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(ios-)`
5. Build Java JNI — same
6. Test .NET — same
7. Test Kotlin/Native — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(ios-) && !startsWith(windows-) && !startsWith(linux-arm64-)`
8. Test Rust — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(ios-)`
9. Test Swift — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(macos-)`
10. Test Zig — `!startsWith(ios-)`
11. Build LWJGL example — `contains(vulkan)`
12. Test Rust example — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(ios-)`
13. Build Swift example — `env == 'macos-arm64-metal'`
14. Build Zig example — `env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(ios-)`
15. Run Zig readback — `!startsWith(ios-)`

So the policy is which envs each "component" (binding/example) supports. There are some interesting patterns:
- ios-* : only build native (no tests, no bindings)
- macos-arm64-egl : excluded from most (a "soft" / less-tested variant — it's the host-default on macos for metal though... wait, no, the default is metal on macos). Actually egl on macos uses ANGLE which is finnicky.
- The native library build/test distinction is by ios.
- LWJGL is vulkan-only.
- Swift binding/example is macos-only.
- Kotlin/Native excludes windows and linux-arm64.

Now, the question is: where should the "manifest" of CI target support policy live?

The task says: "Make it declarative, generate the checked-in workflow from manifests, and enforce that it stays in sync. Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy."

The cleanest approach that fits this repo's existing patterns:

1. The variant manifests already exist as `.mise/config.<env>.toml`. These define *what variants exist*.
2. We need a manifest that declares, per component (binding/example), which envs it supports in CI, plus the runner mapping per env.

I think the cleanest design is:
- A single declarative manifest (e.g., `ci-matrix.toml` or similar) that lists:
  - Each CI target (env) and its runner.
  - Each "test step" (component) and the set of envs it runs on (or a predicate).
- A generator script that reads the manifest + variant configs and emits `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (or the variants job portion).
- A check that the checked-in workflow matches what the generator would produce (enforced in CI hygiene job).

Let me think about whether to ask the user a scoping question. The task says "You may use `ask_user` for consequential architecture or scope questions." 

Key decisions:
1. **Where do the manifests live and what format?** Options: TOML (consistent with mise). I'll go with TOML since the repo is TOML-heavy.
2. **What language for the generator?** The repo uses mise (tasks in bash), Python (uv, pyproject.toml), Node/pnpm (docs), Zig, Rust, etc. Python is a natural fit for a generator since there's `pyproject.toml` and `uv`. Let me check what's in pyproject.toml and scripts/.
3. **How much of the workflow to generate?** Just the `variants` job? Or the whole file? Generating the whole file is cleaner for "stays in sync" enforcement, but the hygiene/docs/required jobs are static. I think generating the `variants` matrix + steps is the core, but to enforce "stays in sync" robustly, generating the whole file from a template + manifest is cleanest. Actually, a good middle ground: generate the entire `ci.yml` from a template so that any drift is caught.
4. **Enforcement mechanism:** A CI check that regenerates and diffs (the hygiene job already does `git diff --exit-code` for lockfile drift). I can add a similar check, or fold it into `ci:lint`.

Let me check scripts/ and pyproject.toml to understand the existing Python setup and conventions.

Let me look more carefully before deciding. Let me check:
- pyproject.toml
- scripts/
- .mise/tasks/
- hk.pkl (linter config) — to see how checks are wired
- actionlint usage

Let me also reconsider the "clearly duplicated policy" part. Is there duplicated policy?

Looking at the variant configs, e.g. `MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND`. The conditions in CI like `contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan')` for LWJGL correspond to render backend. The conditions like `startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-')` correspond to target triple being iOS. So in principle, the CI conditions *could* be derived from variant attributes (render backend, target triple, OS). 

But the task says "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy." So I should be conservative and keep the explicit per-component env lists, rather than trying to derive them from attributes, because the conditions involve nuanced exclusions (macos-arm64-egl excluded from java/rust/etc, kotlin-native excludes windows+linux-arm64). These are policy decisions, not mechanical derivations. So I'll keep them as explicit declarations.

However, there IS clearly duplicated policy: the runner mapping and the list of envs could be cross-checked against the actual `.mise/config.*.toml` files. That's a good "stays in sync" enforcement: the manifest's env list should match the set of config files. Also, I could derive the runner from a per-env declaration.

Let me reconsider the architecture. I think the best design:

**Manifest file** (e.g., `.github/ci-targets.toml` or `.mise/ci-targets.toml`):
- Lists CI targets: each env with its runner and whether it's build-only (ios).
- Lists components (native test, each binding, each example) with their supported envs.

Actually, to reduce duplication and keep it "declarative" and "from manifests", let me think about what the single source of truth should be.

Two levels of manifest:
1. **Variant existence**: `.mise/config.<env>.toml` files are already the source of truth for which variants exist. The generator should read these to know the universe of envs. (Enforcement: CI matrix must include exactly these envs.)
2. **CI target policy**: A new manifest declaring, per env, the runner; and per component, the supported envs.

Hmm, but actually combining: maybe each `.mise/config.<env>.toml` could carry a `[ci]` section with `runner`. That would make the runner co-located with the variant. That's elegant and reduces a separate mapping. Let me consider: the config files are loaded by mise as env config; adding a `[ci]` table wouldn't interfere with mise (mise ignores unknown tables? Actually mise might warn). Let me check — mise config files use `[env]`, `[tasks]`, `[tools]`, etc. Adding a `[ci]` table... mise might error on unknown config. Risky. Better to keep CI policy in a separate manifest.

Let me reconsider. I'll go with a dedicated manifest. Let me decide between:
- Option A: One manifest file `.github/ci.toml` with everything (envs+runners, components+support).
- Option B: Per-component manifest: each binding/example's `mise.toml` declares its CI support, plus a central env->runner map.

Option B is more "distributed" and arguably more maintainable (co-located), and aligns with "generate from manifests" (plural). The component mise.toml files already exist per component. Adding a `[ci]` or `[metadata]` section to each describing supported envs would co-locate policy with the component. But that risks mise parsing issues again, and fragments the policy.

Actually, let me test whether mise tolerates unknown tables in config files. The root mise.toml has `[monorepo]`, `[settings]`, `[hooks]` — these are all mise-recognized. Let me not assume.

Given the constraints and to keep it robust and reviewable, I'll go with **Option A but split logically**: a single declarative manifest that is the source of truth for CI target policy, plus reading the variant config files to validate env existence.

Hmm, wait. Let me reconsider the whole thing once more. Let me re-read the task:

"Our CI target support policy is trapped in a hand-written workflow and keeps drifting. Make it declarative, generate the checked-in workflow from manifests, and enforce that it stays in sync. Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy. Keep normal contributor workflow simple, test the generator, and commit the finished change."

Key phrases:
- "CI target support policy" → which targets (envs) each thing supports.
- "trapped in a hand-written workflow" → ci.yml conditions.
- "Make it declarative" → manifests.
- "generate the checked-in workflow from manifests" → generator produces ci.yml.
- "enforce that it stays in sync" → CI check that ci.yml matches generator output.
- "Preserve current CI behavior unless it is clearly duplicated policy" → behavior preservation, remove only clear duplication.
- "Keep normal contributor workflow simple" → don't make contributors run extra steps; mise-based.
- "test the generator" → unit tests for the generator.
- "commit the finished change" → git commit at the end.

So the design:
1. Manifest(s) declaring CI target policy.
2. Generator (tested) that reads manifests and emits ci.yml.
3. Enforcement: hygiene/lint step regenerates and diffs.
4. Contributor workflow: just edit manifests + run a mise task to regenerate; lint catches drift.

This is a clear, self-consistent design. I don't think I need to ask the user a scoping question — the path is clear and asking would be over-cautious. But let me consider one consequential decision: **how much of ci.yml to generate**. 

Decision: Generate the entire `ci.yml`. Rationale: to truly "enforce it stays in sync", the whole file should be generated so any hand-edit is caught. I'll use a template approach within the generator (the static parts — hygiene, docs, required jobs, triggers — live in the generator/template). This keeps a single source of truth. The generator is tested.

Actually, generating the whole file means the static structure (job names, checkout steps, setup action) is in the generator. That's fine and is common (e.g., many repos generate workflows). The generator becomes the source of truth for the whole file. Tests verify the output is valid YAML and has expected structure.

Let me reconsider: should I preserve the exact `ci.yml` byte-for-byte (including the `# linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host` comments)? The task says preserve behavior. Comments about *why* an exclusion exists are valuable documentation. I should preserve those as part of the manifest (each component's exclusion can carry a `reason` that gets emitted as a comment). That's nice — it makes the manifest self-documenting and the generated workflow retains the rationale.

Now let me look at the existing scripts dir, pyproject.toml, .mise/tasks, and hk.pkl to wire things up idiomatically.

Let me gather all this in parallel.

I have a clear picture of the policy. The CI `variants` job encodes two things that drift: the env→runner matrix and per-component support conditions. Let me examine the surrounding tooling to wire this up idiomatically.

===== hk.pkl =====
// hk orchestrates checks for pre-commit, `mise run check`, and `mise run fix`.
// Tools used here must be installed by `mise install`.

amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Config.pkl"

import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Builtins.pkl"

local lintSteps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
  ["dprint"] = (Builtins.dprint) {
    // the default batch=false hits the cmd length limit on windows
    batch = true
  }
  ["actionlint"] {
    glob = List(".github/workflows/*.yml", ".github/workflows/*.yaml")
    check = "actionlint {{files}}"
  }
  ["jvl"] {
    glob = List("**/*.json", "**/*.jsonc")
    check = "jvl check {{files}}"
  }
  // Mirrors Builtins.`vp-check` (available in hk 1.46+). dprint owns formatting.
  ["vp-check"] {
    glob = List(
      "**/*.js",
      "**/*.mjs",
      "**/*.cjs",
      "**/*.ts",
      "**/*.mts",
      "**/*.cts",
      "**/*.jsx",
      "**/*.tsx",
      "**/*.vue",
      "**/*.svelte",
      "**/*.astro",
    )
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt {{files}}"
    fix = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt --fix {{files}}"
  }
  ["ruff"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ruff check {{files}} && uv run ruff format --check {{files}}"
    fix = "uv run ruff check --fix {{files}} && uv run ruff format {{files}}"
  }
  ["ty"] {
    glob = List("**/*.py")
    exclude = List("third_party/**")
    check = "uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise"
  }
}

hooks {
  ["pre-commit"] {
    fix = true
    stash = "git"
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["check"] {
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
  ["fix"] {
    fix = true
    steps {
      ...lintSteps
    }
  }
}

===== setup-ci-deps =====
name: Setup CI deps
description: Install sccache and mise toolchain

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Enable Git long paths
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: git config --global core.longpaths true

    - name: Cache MapLibre Native source
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          third_party/maplibre-native
          .git/modules/third_party/maplibre-native
        key: mln-src-v2-${{ hashFiles('.gitmodules', 'cmake/mln_version.cmake') }}

    - name: Install sccache
      uses: mozilla-actions/sccache-action@9e7fa8a12102821edf02ca5dbea1acd0f89a2696 # v0.0.10

    - name: Use Linux software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends --yes \
          libegl-mesa0 \
          libgl1-mesa-dri \
          libvulkan1 \
          mesa-utils \
          mesa-utils-extra \
          mesa-vulkan-drivers
        {
          echo "EGL_PLATFORM=surfaceless"
          echo "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=true"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Use Windows software graphics drivers
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $mesaVersion = "26.0.8"
        $mesaArchive = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d.7z"
        $mesaRoot = Join-Path $env:RUNNER_TEMP "mesa3d"
        $mesaUrl = "https://github.com/pal1000/mesa-dist-win/releases/download/$mesaVersion/mesa3d-$mesaVersion-release-msvc.7z"

        Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $mesaUrl -OutFile $mesaArchive
        $expectedHash = "A438C26C2752726916E455F9AD121F8A7E3CFECF8626251ABF8E5B3E129D8497"
        $actualHash = (Get-FileHash $mesaArchive -Algorithm SHA256).Hash
        if ($actualHash -ne $expectedHash) {
          throw "Mesa archive hash mismatch: expected $expectedHash, got $actualHash"
        }
        7z x $mesaArchive "-o$mesaRoot" -y | Out-Null

        $mesaWglDriver = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "libgallium_wgl.dll" |
          Where-Object { $_.FullName -match "x64" } |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $mesaWglDriver) {
          throw "Missing Mesa WGL driver in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $driverManifest = Get-ChildItem $mesaRoot -Recurse -Filter "lvp_icd.x86_64.json" |
          Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $driverManifest) {
          throw "Missing lavapipe Vulkan ICD manifest in $mesaArchive"
        }

        $mesaBin = $mesaWglDriver.Directory.FullName
        $mesaWglSystemDriver = Join-Path $env:WINDIR "System32\mesadrv.dll"
        Copy-Item $mesaWglDriver.FullName $mesaWglSystemDriver -Force

        $openglDriverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\OpenGLDrivers\MSOGL"
        New-Item -Path $openglDriverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DLL -PropertyType String -Value "mesadrv.dll" -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name DriverVersion -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Flags -PropertyType DWord -Value 1 -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $openglDriverKey -Name Version -PropertyType DWord -Value 2 -Force | Out-Null

        $driverKey = "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Khronos\Vulkan\Drivers"
        New-Item -Path $driverKey -Force | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path $driverKey -Name $driverManifest.FullName -PropertyType DWord -Value 0 -Force | Out-Null

        "GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV
        "Path=$mesaBin;$env:Path" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Cache Cargo packages
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.cargo/registry/index/
          ~/.cargo/registry/cache/
          ~/.cargo/git/db/
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-cargo-registry-v1-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}

    - name: Cache Gradle
      uses: actions/cache@27d5ce7f107fe9357f9df03efb73ab90386fccae # v5.0.5
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.gradle/caches
          ~/.gradle/wrapper
        key: ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle*', '**/gradle-wrapper.properties') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.os }}-${{ runner.arch }}-gradle-

    - name: Trim Windows PATH before mise activation
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Windows' }}
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $workspaceDrive = Split-Path -Qualifier $env:GITHUB_WORKSPACE
        # Git Bash maps /tmp to the checkout drive; create it before mise hooks run.
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $workspaceDrive "tmp") | Out-Null

        $paths = @(
          if ($env:SCCACHE_PATH) { Split-Path -Parent $env:SCCACHE_PATH }
          if ($env:Path) { $env:Path -split ';' }
          "C:\Program Files\Git\bin",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32",
          "$env:SystemRoot",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\Wbem",
          "$env:SystemRoot\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0",
          "C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7",
          "C:\Program Files\Git\cmd"
        ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }

        "Path=$($paths -join ';')" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

        $gradleTemp = $env:RUNNER_TEMP -replace '\\', '/'
        $gradleOptions = "-Djava.io.tmpdir=$gradleTemp"
        if ($env:GRADLE_OPTS) {
          $gradleOptions = "$gradleOptions $env:GRADLE_OPTS"
        }
        "GRADLE_OPTS=$gradleOptions" >> $env:GITHUB_ENV

    - name: Install mise toolchain
      uses: jdx/mise-action@1648a7812b9aeae629881980618f079932869151 # v4.0.1
      with:
        cache: true
        cache_key_prefix: mise-v2
        install: true

    - name: Wire sccache into CMake
      shell: bash
      run: |
        {
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=true"
          echo "SCCACHE_GHA_VERSION=${MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE}"
          echo "SCCACHE_BASEDIRS=${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}"
          echo "CMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
          echo "CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=sccache"
        } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

    - name: Diagnose EGL setup
      if: ${{ runner.os == 'Linux' }}
      shell: bash
      run: |
        mise exec -- bash -euo pipefail -c '
          env | sort | grep -E "^(EGL|LIBGL|MESA|LD_LIBRARY_PATH|__EGL|MLN_FFI|VK_)" || true
          command -v eglinfo
          EGL_LOG_LEVEL=debug LIBGL_DEBUG=verbose LD_DEBUG=libs eglinfo || true
        '

    - name: Diagnose Vulkan setup
      if: ${{ contains(env.MISE_ENV, 'vulkan') }}
      shell: bash
      run: mise run ci:vulkan-info

    # The mise action may restore Rust metadata without all rustup-managed
    # component files. Repair the toolchain only when rustup cannot add the
    # configured components or the component binaries still cannot run.
    - name: Ensure Rust components
      shell: bash
      run: |
        ensure_rust_components() {
          mise exec -- rustup component add rustfmt clippy &&
            mise exec -- cargo fmt --version &&
            mise exec -- cargo clippy --version
        }

        if ! ensure_rust_components; then
          mise install --force rust
          ensure_rust_components
        fi

part.text:
No mise, no uv, no ruff available directly in this environment. The repo's pinned Python is 3.14 via mise. Let me check what's available. The environment has python3 3.12.3 at /opt/venv. Let me check if I can install uv or if there's a way to run the generator.

Let me check the AI_POLICY.md too since it's referenced, and think about the testing approach.

```toml
# CI target support policy.
#
# This file is the source of truth for which build variants run in CI and which
# mise tasks each variant runs. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is generated from it
# by `scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py` (run `mise run ci:generate-workflow`).
# CI fails if the checked-in workflow drifts from this manifest.
#
# Variant identifiers must match the `.mise/config.<variant>.toml` files exactly;
# the generator cross-checks the two so neither can drift unnoticed.

# GitHub Actions runner for each variant.
[runners]
linux-arm64-egl = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
linux-arm64-vulkan = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
linux-x64-egl = "ubuntu-latest"
linux-x64-vulkan = "ubuntu-latest"
macos-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
macos-arm64-vulkan = "macos-latest"
macos-arm64-egl = "macos-latest"
ios-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
ios-simulator-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
windows-x64-vulkan = "windows-2022"
windows-x64-wgl = "windows-2022"

# CI steps. Each step runs a mise task on the listed variants.
#   task   — mise task id (the step runs `mise run <task>`).
#   name   — human-readable step name shown in the Actions log.
#   envs   — explicit allow-list of variants the step runs on.
#   reason — optional note rendered as a comment explaining exclusions.

name = "Configure"
task = "configure"
envs = [...all...]

task = "build"
envs = ["ios-arm64-metal", "ios-simulator-arm64-metal"]

...
```

```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          ...
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["ios-arm64-metal","ios-simulator-arm64-metal"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run build
      ...

  required:
    ...
```

# CI target support policy.
#
# Source of truth for the `variants` job in .github/workflows/ci.yml: which build
# variants run in CI, which GitHub runner each uses, and which mise task each
# variant runs. The workflow is generated from this file by
# scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py:
#
#   mise run ci:generate-workflow
#
# CI fails (via `mise run ci:check-workflow`) if the checked-in workflow drifts
# from this manifest. Variant identifiers must each match a
# `.mise/config.<variant>.toml` file; the generator cross-checks the two sets so
# neither can drift unnoticed.
#
# Steps appear in workflow display order. Each step field means:
#   name    step name shown in the Actions log (omit for an unnamed `run` step)
#   task    mise task id; the step runs `mise run <task>`
#   envs    variants the step runs on, as an explicit allow-list, or "all"
#   reason  optional rationale lines, rendered as workflow comments

# GitHub Actions runner selected for each build variant.
[runners]
linux-arm64-egl = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
linux-arm64-vulkan = "ubuntu-24.04-arm"
linux-x64-egl = "ubuntu-latest"
linux-x64-vulkan = "ubuntu-latest"
macos-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
macos-arm64-vulkan = "macos-latest"
macos-arm64-egl = "macos-latest"
ios-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
ios-simulator-arm64-metal = "macos-latest"
windows-x64-vulkan = "windows-2022"
windows-x64-wgl = "windows-2022"

# Native library: iOS variants cross-compile (build only); everything else tests.
[[steps]]
task = "configure"
envs = "all"

[[steps]]
name = "Build native library"
task = "build"
envs = ["ios-arm64-metal", "ios-simulator-arm64-metal"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test native library"
task = "test"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-egl",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

# Bindings. macOS EGL is excluded: it relies on a vendored ANGLE build that is
# not exercised by the bindings. iOS is build-only (no host to run tests).
[[steps]]
name = "Build Java FFM binding"
task = "//bindings/java-ffm:build"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Java JNI binding"
task = "//bindings/java-jni:build"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Test .NET binding"
task = "//bindings/dotnet:test"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Kotlin/Native binding"
task = "//bindings/kotlin-native:build"
reason = [
  "linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871",
  "windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446",
]
envs = ["linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Rust binding"
task = "//bindings/rust:ci"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Swift binding"
task = "//bindings/swift:test"
envs = ["macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan"]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Zig binding"
task = "//bindings/zig:ci"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-egl",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

# Examples.
[[steps]]
name = "Build LWJGL example"
task = "//examples/lwjgl-map:build"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Test Rust example"
task = "//examples/rust-map:ci"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Swift example"
task = "//examples/swift-map:build"
envs = ["macos-arm64-metal"]

[[steps]]
name = "Build Zig example"
task = "//examples/zig-map:build"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-vulkan",
  "windows-x64-wgl",
]

[[steps]]
name = "Run Zig readback example"
task = "//examples/zig-readback:run"
envs = [
  "linux-arm64-egl",
  "linux-arm64-vulkan",
  "linux-x64-egl",
  "linux-x64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-metal",
  "macos-arm64-vulkan",
  "macos-arm64-egl",

]

"""Generate ``.github/workflows/ci.yml`` from ``.github/ci-targets.toml``.

The CI target support policy (which build variants run in CI, on which runners,
and which mise task each variant runs) is declared in
``.github/ci-targets.toml``. This module renders that policy into the checked-in
GitHub Actions workflow.

Usage::

    mise run ci:generate-workflow   # write .github/workflows/ci.yml

CI verifies the workflow stays in sync via ``mise run ci:check-workflow``.

The generator cross-checks the manifest against the ``.mise/config.<variant>.toml``
files so a variant cannot exist in CI without a config (or vice versa).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import sys
import tomllib
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
MANIFEST_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "ci-targets.toml"
VARIANT_CONFIG_DIR = REPO_ROOT / ".mise"
WORKFLOW_PATH = REPO_ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"

CHECKOUT_REF = "df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3"

GENERATED_HEADER = (
    "# This file is generated from .github/ci-targets.toml by\n"
    "# scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py. Do not edit by hand; run\n"
    "# `mise run ci:generate-workflow` and commit the result.\n"
)


class ManifestError(ValueError):
    """Raised when the CI target manifest is structurally invalid."""


def load_manifest(path: Path = MANIFEST_PATH) -> dict:
    """Parse the CI target manifest, returning a plain dict."""
    with path.open("rb") as handle:
        return tomllib.load(handle)


def discover_variant_configs(variant_dir: Path = VARIANT_CONFIG_DIR) -> list[str]:
    """Return variant names backed by a ``.mise/config.<variant>.toml`` file."""
    names = []
    for config in sorted(variant_dir.glob("config.*.toml")):
        # config.<variant>.toml -> <variant>
        stem = config.name[len("config.") : -len(".toml")]
        names.append(stem)
    return names


def resolve_envs(step: dict, all_envs: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Resolve a step's ``envs`` to an ordered list of variants.

    ``"all"`` expands to every variant; an explicit list is reordered to match
    the canonical variant order (the manifest's ``[runners]`` order).
    """
    raw = step["envs"]
    if raw == "all":
        return list(all_envs)
    order = {env: i for i, env in enumerate(all_envs)}
    return sorted(raw, key=lambda env: order[env])


def validate(manifest: dict, variant_configs: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Validate the manifest and return the canonical variant order.

    Raises :class:`ManifestError` on any inconsistency.
    """
    if "runners" not in manifest:
        raise ManifestError("manifest is missing the [runners] table")
    runners: dict = manifest["runners"]
    if not runners:
        raise ManifestError("[runners] must list at least one variant")

    all_envs = list(runners.keys())

    # Every variant must be backed by a mise config file, and vice versa.
    runner_set = set(all_envs)
    config_set = set(variant_configs)
    missing_configs = sorted(runner_set - config_set)
    extra_configs = sorted(config_set - runner_set)
    if missing_configs:
        raise ManifestError(
            "variants in [runners] without a .mise/config.<variant>.toml: "
            + ", ".join(missing_configs)
        )
    if extra_configs:
        raise ManifestError(
            "variant configs without an entry in [runners]: "
            + ", ".join(extra_configs)
        )

    # Validate steps.
    steps = manifest.get("steps", [])
    if not steps:
        raise ManifestError("manifest must define at least one [[steps]] entry")
    seen_tasks: set[str] = set()
    for index, step in enumerate(steps):
        location = f"step #{index + 1}"
        if "task" not in step:
            raise ManifestError(f"{location} is missing a 'task'")
        task = step["task"]
        if task in seen_tasks:
            raise ManifestError(f"{location} reuses task {task!r}")
        seen_tasks.add(task)
        if "envs" not in step:
            raise ManifestError(f"{location} ({task}) is missing 'envs'")
        envs = resolve_envs(step, all_envs)
        unknown = sorted(set(envs) - runner_set)
        if unknown:
            raise ManifestError(
                f"{location} ({task}) lists unknown variants: {', '.join(unknown)}"
            )
        if not envs:
            raise ManifestError(f"{location} ({task}) runs on no variants")

    return all_envs


def _contains_condition(envs: list[str]) -> str:
    """Build the ``contains(fromJson(...), matrix.mise_env)`` expression."""
    json_list = "[" + ", ".join(f'"{env}"' for env in envs) + "]"
    return f"contains(fromJson('{json_list}'), matrix.mise_env)"


def _render_matrix_include(runners: dict) -> list[str]:
    lines = ["        include:"]
    for env, runner in runners.items():
        lines.append(f"          - mise_env: {env}")
        lines.append(f"            runner: {runner}")
    return lines


def _render_step(step: dict, all_envs: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Render a single workflow step (reason comment + keys)."""
    envs = resolve_envs(step, all_envs)
    task = step["task"]
    name = step.get("name")
    reason = step.get("reason", [])

    lines = [f"      # {line}" for line in reason]

    fields: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    if name:
        fields.append(("name", name))
    if set(envs) != set(all_envs):
        fields.append(("if", f"${{({_contains_condition(envs)})}}"))
    fields.append(("run", f"mise run {task}"))

    for index, (key, value) in enumerate(fields):
        prefix = "      - " if index == 0 else "        "
        lines.append(f"{prefix}{key}: {value}")
    return lines


def _static_jobs() -> list[str]:
    """Return the non-generated jobs and workflow scaffolding as YAML lines."""
    return [
        f"      - uses: actions/checkout@{CHECKOUT_REF}",
        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
    ]


def render(manifest: dict, all_envs: list[str]) -> str:
    """Render the full workflow YAML text from a validated manifest."""
    runners = manifest["runners"]
    steps = manifest["steps"]

    lines: list[str] = []
    lines.extend(GENERATED_HEADER.rstrip("\n").split("\n"))
    lines.extend(
        [
            "",
            "name: CI",
            "",
            "on:",
            "  push:",
            "    branches: [main]",
            "  pull_request:",
            "    branches: [main]",
            "",
            "permissions:",
            "  contents: read",
            "",
            "concurrency:",
            "  group:"
            " ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number ||"
            " github.ref }}",
            "  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}",
            "",
            "jobs:",
            "  hygiene:",
            "    name: hygiene",
            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
            "    timeout-minutes: 30",
            "    steps:",
            f"      - uses: actions/checkout@{CHECKOUT_REF}",
            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
            "      - run: mise run ci:lint",
            "",
            "  docs:",
            "    name: docs",
            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
            "    timeout-minutes: 60",
            "    steps:",
            f"      - uses: actions/checkout@{CHECKOUT_REF}",
            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
            "      - run: mise run //docs:build",
            "",
            "  variants:",
            "    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
            "    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}",
            "    timeout-minutes: 120",
            "    env:",
            "      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
            "    strategy:",
            "      fail-fast: false",
            "      matrix:",
        ]
    )
    lines.extend(_render_matrix_include(runners))
    lines.extend(
        [
            "    steps:",
            f"      - uses: actions/checkout@{CHECKOUT_REF}",
            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
            "",
        ]
    )
    for step in steps:
        lines.extend(_render_step(step, all_envs))
    lines.extend(
        [
            "",
            "  required:",
            "    name: ci-required",
            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
            "    timeout-minutes: 5",
            "    if: ${{ always() }}",
            "    needs:",
            "      - hygiene",
            "      - docs",
            "      - variants",
            "    steps:",
            "      - name: Fail if required jobs failed",
            "        if:"
            " ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') ||"
            " contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') ||"
            " contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
            "        run: |",
            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
            "          exit 1",
            "",
            "      - run: true",
            "",
        ]
    )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def generate(
    manifest_path: Path = MANIFEST_PATH,
    variant_dir: Path = VARIANT_CONFIG_DIR,
    workflow_path: Path = WORKFLOW_PATH,
) -> str:
    """Validate the manifest and (re)write the workflow, returning its text."""
    manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
    variant_configs = discover_variant_configs(variant_dir)
    all_envs = validate(manifest, variant_configs)
    text = render(manifest, all_envs)
    workflow_path.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")
    return text


def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
    try:
        text = generate()
    except (ManifestError, OSError) as error:
        print(f"error: {error}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1
    print(f"wrote {WORKFLOW_PATH.relative_to(REPO_ROOT)} ({len(text)} bytes)")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main(sys.argv[1:]))

part.text:
Wait, I have an unused function `_static_jobs()` that returns just the checkout+setup lines. That's dead code / not used (ruff F811? no, it's defined but unused → ruff doesn't flag unused functions by default; F-rules flag unused imports/variables, not unused functions). But it's dead code — campsite rule says leave tidy. Let me remove it. Also I have `from __future__ import annotations` — used? It enables PEP 563 but with no annotations that need deferring... I do use `dict`, `list[str]`, `Path` annotations. With `from __future__ import annotations` they're strings; without it, `list[str]` works at runtime in 3.9+. Either way fine. But is it "unused"? No, it's a valid import. ruff won't flag it. Keep it (idiomatic). Actually, let me remove `_static_jobs` since it's unused dead code.

Also, let me reconsider the concurrency line construction — I split it awkwardly across lines for the 88-col limit:
```python
"  group:"
" ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number ||"
" github.ref }}",
```
This produces: `  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}`. Let me verify: Python adjacent string literals concatenate. `"  group:" + " ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number ||" + " github.ref }}"` = `  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}`. Good. But this is ugly and the concatenated result is a long YAML line (~78 chars) which is fine for YAML. Since ci.yml is excluded from dprint, line length doesn't matter for formatting. But for readability of the GENERATOR source, the split is to keep generator lines ≤88 (ruff format line length 88). Let me check the generator line lengths are all ≤88. The split strings each ≤88. Good.

Actually, since ci.yml is excluded from dprint, I could write the YAML lines long without worrying about dprint. But the GENERATOR python lines must be ≤88 for ruff format. So I split the long string literals. That's what I did. Fine.

Let me reconsider: instead of awkward string concatenation, I could write the long YAML line as a single string literal and let ruff format keep it (ruff format doesn't split string literals by default — it leaves long strings alone). So `"...long line..."` > 88 chars would NOT be split by ruff format (ruff doesn't break strings). But ruff CHECK (E501) is not in default select, so no error. So a single long string literal is fine for ruff! Let me simplify by using single long string literals where the YAML line is long, instead of concatenation. That's much more readable.

Wait — does ruff format break long strings? No. ruff format does not wrap string literals. So `"  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}"` (a ~78-char string literal, plus indentation and quotes ~ 84 chars on that source line) — the SOURCE line would be ~84 chars, under 88. Fine, no split needed. Let me simplify.

Let me check the longest YAML lines:
- `  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}` → with `"..."` and 8-space list indent: `        "  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}",` — let me count: 8 (indent) + 1 (quote) + 78 (content) + 1 (quote) + 1 (comma) = 89. Slightly over 88! So this one source line is 89 chars → E501 not enforced (not in default select), and ruff format won't break it. So it's fine for ruff. But it's 1 char over the 88 "preferred." Ruff format won't touch it. Acceptable. But to be clean, I could split. Hmm.

Actually, let me reconsider whether to even format-check the generator. ruff format WILL run (hk fix). If a source line is 89 chars, ruff format leaves it (doesn't split strings, doesn't enforce E501). So stable. Good. I'll use single literals but verify each is stable under ruff format. The only things ruff format changes: indentation, trailing commas, quote style, blank lines. So as long as I write 4-space indent, double quotes, trailing commas in multiline, I'm stable.

Let me rewrite the render function more cleanly with single-line string literals, and remove dead code. Let me also double check the `if:` expression rendering: `fields.append(("if", f"${{({_contains_condition(envs)})}}"))`. `_contains_condition` returns `contains(fromJson('["a","b"]'), matrix.mise_env)`. So the if line becomes `        if: ${{(contains(fromJson('["a","b"]'), matrix.mise_env)}}`. 

Hmm, original format is `if: ${{ contains(...) }}` with spaces inside `${{ }}`. Mine produces `${{(...)}}` with parens right inside. Both are valid. But to match original style (and actionlint friendliness), let me produce `${{ contains(...) }}` with spaces. Let me adjust: `f"if: ${{ {_contains_condition(envs)} }}"`. That gives `if: ${{ contains(fromJson(...), matrix.mise_env) }}`. 

Let me verify the f-string escaping: in an f-string, `{{` → `{` and `}}` → `}`. So `f"${{ {x} }}"` → `${{ <x> }}`? No: `f"${{ {x} }}"` → `$` + `{` (from `{{`) + ` ` + value + ` ` + `}` (from `}}`) = `${ value }`. Wait that gives `${ value }` not `${{ value }}`. I need `${{ value }}` (double braces for GitHub expression). So I need 4 braces on each side in the f-string: `f"${{{{ {x} }}}}"`? Let me count: to output literal `${{`, I need `$` then `{{{{` → produces `{{`. So `f"${{{{ {x} }}}}"` → `$` + `{{` + ` ` + x + ` ` + `}}` = `${{ x }}`. Yes. That's 5 braces each side: `${{{{` = `$` + `{{{{` → `$` + `{{`. And `}}}}}` → `}}` + `}`? No. Let me be careful.

f-string brace rules: `{{` → `{`, `}}` → `}`, and `{expr}` → value.

To produce `${{ expr }}`:
- `$` literal
- `{` literal → `{{`
- `{` literal → `{{`
- ` ` literal
- expr → `{x}`
- ` ` literal
- `}` literal → `}}`
- `}` literal → `}}`

So: `f"$` + `{{` + `{{` + ` {x} ` + `}}` + `}}` = `f"${{{{ {x} }}}}"`. Counting braces after `$`: `{{{{` = two `{{` pairs = two literal `{`. Good. Then ` {x} `. Then `}}}}` = two `}}` pairs = two literal `}`. So the full f-string: `f"${{{{ {x} }}}}"`. Let me verify char by char: `$`, `{`,`{`,`{`,`{`,` `,`{`,`x`,`}`,` `,`}`,`}`,`}`,`}`. Parse: `$` literal. `{{`→`{`. `{{`→`{`. ` ` literal. `{x}`→value. ` ` literal. `}}`→`}`. `}}`→`}`. Result: `$`+`{`+`{`+` `+value+` `+`}`+`}` = `${{ value }}`. 

This is hard to read. Let me avoid f-string brace hell by building with concatenation or .format-free approach:
```python
if_expr = "${{ " + _contains_condition(envs) + " }}"
fields.append(("if", if_expr))
```
Much clearer. `"${{ "` is a normal string (no f), so `{{` is literal `{`? No! In a NORMAL (non-f) string, `{{` is just two characters `{` `{`. There's no escaping in normal strings. So `"${{ "` is literally `${{ ` (dollar, two braces, space). And `" }}"` is ` }}` (space, two braces). So `if_expr = "${{ " + cond + " }}"` = `${{ cond }}`. 

So use normal string concatenation, not f-strings, for the brace parts. Clean and correct. Let me use that.

Similarly the matrix include and other `${{ ... }}` lines: e.g. `"    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}"` — this is a NORMAL string, so `${{` is literal 3 chars and `}}` literal 2 chars. Correct as-is. Good, no f-string needed there.

For lines with f-string substitution AND `${{ }}`: e.g. `f"          - mise_env: {env}"` — no literal braces needed, fine. And `runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}` is a normal string, fine.

So the only tricky one is the `if:` field, which I'll build via concatenation. Good.

Let me also reconsider the concurrency group line — it's a normal string with `${{ }}`:
`"  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}"` — normal string, `${{ }}` literal. Length: let me count the source line with indentation (8 spaces) + quotes: 8 + 1 + len + 1 + 1(comma). The content `  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}` = let me count: "  group: " (9) + "${{ github.workflow }}" (21) + "-" (1) + "${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}" (49) = 80. Plus 8 indent + 2 quotes + 1 comma = 91. Over 88. Ruff format won't split it (string literal). ruff check E501 not enforced. So stable but 91 chars. Acceptable since ruff won't touch it. But it's a bit long for taste. 

Alternatively, I can keep the split concatenation for just these two long lines to keep generator source ≤88. But split concatenation is ugly. Hmm. Since ruff is fine either way, I'll keep single literals for readability and accept ~91 char source lines. Ruff format leaves long strings alone → stable. Good.

Wait, but actually I want to be SURE ruff format doesn't try to wrap. ruff format does NOT wrap string literals or enforce line length on string content. Confirmed behavior. So single long string literals are stable. 

Now let me also remove the unused `_static_jobs` function and the awkward concatenations, rewriting render cleanly.

Let me rewrite the generator cleanly.
Let me clean up the generator — remove dead code and simplify brace handling using plain string concatenation (cleaner than f-string brace escaping):

    lines.extend(

            " contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
            "        run: |",
            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
            "          exit 1",
            "",
            "      - run: true",
            "",
        ]
    )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def generate(
    manifest_path: Path = MANIFEST_PATH,
    variant_dir: Path = VARIANT_CONFIG_DIR,
    workflow_path: Path = WORKFLOW_PATH,
) -> str:
    """Validate the manifest and (re)write the workflow, returning its text."""
    manifest = load_manifest(manifest_path)
    variant_configs = discover_variant_configs(variant_dir)
    all_envs = validate(manifest, variant_configs)
    text = render(manifest, all_envs)
    workflow_path.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")

    envs = resolve_envs(step, all_envs)

=== generated ci.yml ===
# This file is generated from .github/ci-targets.toml by
# scripts/generate_ci_workflow.py. Do not edit by hand; run
# `mise run ci:generate-workflow` and commit the result.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["ios-arm64-metal", "ios-simulator-arm64-metal"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-egl", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run test
      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-egl", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci
      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["macos-arm64-metal"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ contains(fromJson('["linux-arm64-egl", "linux-arm64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl", "linux-x64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-metal", "macos-arm64-vulkan", "macos-arm64-egl", "windows-x64-vulkan", "windows-x64-wgl"]'), matrix.mise_env) }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true
